sglang.title: [diffusion] Make warmup image initialization rank-safe
sglang.author: IPostYellow
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
During server startup with --warmup and --warmup-resolutions, warmup request preparation runs on every worker rank.
For image-conditioned tasks (I2I/TI2I/I2V/TI2V), multiple ranks could touch the same warmup image path at startup, which may lead to intermittent image loading failures (PIL

Over

sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Sana (and some other models, including mova) consistency flakiness was caused by duplicate transformer safetensors variants being loaded together, which made final weights depend on loader/streamer ordering.
Modifications

Filter duplicate precision variants in transformer weight resoluti

sglang.title: [sgl-kernel] support > 1024 experts in moe_align_block_size kernel
sglang.author: klshuster
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The existing moe_align_block_size CUDA kernel uses CUB block-level scan primitives that are limited to 1024 threads, which caps the maximum number of experts at 1024. Models with virtual/merged LoRA experts or very large MoE configurations (e.g., 2048 or 4096 experts) hit this limit. This

sglang.title: [GDN] Remove FlashInfer GDN decode + no_buffer guard and default to FlashInfer on SM100+
sglang.author: YAMY1234
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The FlashInfer GDN decode kernel was previously blocked from being used with --mamba-scheduler-strategy no_buffer due to accuracy degradation caused by OOB memory access from negative padding indices in the bf16 decode kernel (see #20791).
The root cause was fixed in FlashInfer v0.6.7 via

sglang.title: Fix hybrid_linear_attn_backend crash with ngram speculation
sglang.author: he-yufeng
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Problem
hybrid_linear_attn_backend accesses spec_info.topk at runtime during target_verify mode, but NgramVerifyInput doesn't define topk, causing an AttributeError crash with --speculative-algo NGRAM.
Fix
Read topk from server_args.speculative_eagle_topk at init time instead of from spec_info at ru

FA4 is written in CuTe DSL, which uses JIT caching so it doesn't require AOT compilation. When installing FA4, cmake just copies the files in the cute/ directory into vllm/vllm_flash_attn. This means that when working on FA4 locally, we need to run the build step before testing, in order for

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Add missing ASRDataset import and CLI args in benchmarks/throughput.py

(e.g. openslr/librispeech_asr).
This PR adds the missing ASRDataset import and the

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix V1 logprobs empty strings for multi-byte UTF-8 tokens when logprobs > 0
vllm.author: haosdent
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fixes #27300
When requesting logprobs > 0 with the V1 engine, byte-fallback tokens (e.g., curly quotes “ / ” which are 3 UTF-8 bytes each) show up as empty strings "" in the top_logprobs decoded token output instead of the correct characters. The logprobs=0 case was already fixed by #28874,

vllm.title: [Core] Use tuple_return in split_module for tuple-conformant subgraphs
vllm.author: frgossen
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Pass tuple_return=True to split_module (torch >= 2.12) so all piecewise
subgraphs return tuples, even when they have a single output value. This
matches the convention expected by compile_fx, which internally normalizes
non-tuple outputs via make_graph_return_tuple before computing cache key

Furthermore, this PR moves to using a (group_idx, block_hash) offload key.

a simple automatic path that works for the common case

Purpose
Do not shard shared experts weights when sequence parallel is enabled to fix precision issue for Qwen3.5/Qwen3-Next with EP. At present, when sequence_parallel is enabled, shared experts would apply tensor parallel and do not do all reduce at the end of calculation, which results in the prec

vllm.title: [torch.compile] Allow usage of Opaque Objects in PyTorch 2.11
vllm.author: zou3519
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We turned this off temporarily in the pt2.11 upgrade because there was some failing tests and I wanted the pt2.11 upgrade to go in first. This PR turns Opaque Objects back on and fixes the tests.
The problem with the tests was that we need to monkeypatch inductor to fix an issue with opaque objects.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] FlashInfer MXINT4 MoE crashes, missing do_finalize
vllm.author: benchislett
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
In FlashInfer 0.6.4 the interface to the fused MoE backends changed. MXINT4 did not get updated. This means that VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_MOE_INT4 has been unusable in vLLM since v0.15.1.
FIX #39245
Test Plan
Launches fine with the flag enabled. GSM8k pass, 1k1k runs ~4% faster with this flag ena

vllm.title: [W8A8 Block Linear Refactor][2/N] Remove W8A8Fp8BlockLinearOp and adopt Fp8 block linear kernel selections.
vllm.author: maralbahari
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR  refactors block scaled linear kernel into kernel abstraction.
changes:

Introduces MMLinearKernel base interface for all linear kernels.
Introduces Params, Fp8Params and Int8Params,  classes to access layer params in structured format.
Introduces DynamicMMLinearKernel which is a typ

vllm.title: [UX] Integrate DeepGEMM into vLLM wheel via CMake
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Bundles DeepGEMM into the vLLM wheel via CMake's FetchContent, so users no longer need to manually run tools/install_deepgemm.sh
Builds DeepGEMM's pybind11 _C extension and vendors the Python package + JIT include headers into vllm/third_party/deep_gemm/
Removes the separate DeepGEMM build/

vllm.title: [Gemma4] Support quantized MoE
vllm.author: dsikka
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose

Expand gemma4 MoE weight loading to include logic for 2D quantized layers and parameters

Testing

Existing MoE checkpoints(without quantization) load without issue
Quantized checkpoints (e.g https://huggingface.co/RedHatAI/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-FP8-Dynamic) are now able to load with this chan

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix cpu-offload-gb assertion with non-default block sizes
vllm.author: AjAnubolu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Remove assertion in may_reinitialize_input_batch that blocked CPU offloading when block sizes differ from defaults. The original issue it guarded against no longer applies.
Closes #36279

vllm.title: [Bug] Fix routing bias dtype for trtllm per-block fp8 moe
vllm.author: wzhao18
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix [Bug]: Deepseek R1 produces incorrect output
FIX #39179
Flashinfer v0.6.7 requires bf16 routing bias dtype for trtllm MoE. We have done this in #38423 for nvfp4 and fp8 per-tensor, but haven't done for fp8 per-block.
Test Plan
Test Result
main:
vllm serve deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1 --trust-

vllm.title: [Model] Update ColModernVBERT to support latest HF checkpoint
vllm.author: ieBoytsov
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Summary

Update ColModernVBERT to support the latest ModernVBERT/colmodernvbert-merged HF checkpoint which changed model_type from colmodernvbert to modernvbert and switched to a flat config layout
Simplify weight mapper and remove legacy DecoupledEmbedding concatenation logic no longer need

vllm.title: Fix Responses JSON schema alias serialization
vllm.author: noobHappylife
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix Responses API JSON Schema alias serialization so streamed response.created and JSON responses emit the public schema field instead of internal schema_, which was breaking non-Harmony /v1/responses tool-calling with tool_choice="required".
Fix #38245 where #38262 doesn't completely fix it

vllm.title: [PD][HeteroArch]Fix accuracy issue with CPU_ATTN as Decoder and Flash_ATTN as prefiller
vllm.author: xuechendi
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix the error reported in #38710
When using CPU as decoder to work with Hetero Arch Platform (CUDA, Intel GPU, Intel Gaudi ...) as prefiller, there is an accuracy issue due to CPU KV layout need addition packing step
In this PR, we fixed this issue by following cpu_attn kv Packing method to

vllm.title: [Refactor] Improve indexer decode path metadata preparation
vllm.author: zyongye
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
What changed
This PR refactors the decode path of DeepseekV32IndexerMetadataBuilder and the supporting C++ kernels. No behavioral changes on any existing path.
vllm/v1/attention/backends/mla/indexer.py

Remove offsets from DeepSeekV32IndexerDecodeMetadata. Per-token effective lengths are now

vllm.title: [Multimodal] Fix nested_tensors_equal: add length check for lists and tuple support
vllm.author: khairulkabir1661
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Fixes a bug in nested_tensors_equal() where lists of different lengths incorrectly compare as equal due to Python's zip() silently truncating to the shorter list. Also adds missing support for tuple comparisons.
Related: INFERENG-5520
Bug Description
The nested_tensors_equal function in vllm

vllm.title: [XPU] Skip VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT for XPU in EAGLE DP test
vllm.author: 1643661061leo
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR disables the forced VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT=1 setting for Intel XPU in test_run_eagle_dp, aligning its behavior with ROCm.
Motivation:
In test_run_eagle_dp, VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT=1 is forcefully set to ensure the tokens generated with and without EAGLE are strictly identical. However, si

vllm.title: [Docker] Add fastsafetensors to NVIDIA Dockerfile
vllm.author: zhewenl
vllm.bodyExcerpt:

Add fastsafetensors = 0.2.2 to requirements/cuda.txt to enable faster safetensors model loading via GPU Direct Storage
Install libnuma-dev in the vllm-base Dockerfile stage as a runtime dependency for fastsafetensors (see #20384)
